title: Aggregate Demand
description: Practice what you've learned about the wealth effect, interest rate effect, exchange rate effect, and the factors that shift aggregate demand (AD) in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the relationship illustrated by the aggregate demand (AD) curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The inverse relationship between the price level and the quantity of aggregate output demanded by households, firms, the government, and the rest of the world
The inverse relationship between the price of a good and the quantity demanded of that good
The positive relationship between the price level and the quantity of aggregate output demanded by households, firms, the government, and the rest of the world
The positive relationship between the price level and the quantity of total output produced by firms and households
It shows that there is no relationship between the price level and the amount of output demanded

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is *not* a component of aggregate demand (AD)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which curve shows the relationship between the price level and the quantity of goods and services demanded by households, firms, government, and the rest of the world?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

Assume that the economy in question is open to international trade.

**The aggregate demand (AD) curve shows the relationship between the price level and the quantities demanded of goods and services by which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Households, firms, the government, and the rest of the world
Households, firms, and the rest of the world
Households, the government, and the rest of the world
Households, firms, the government, the rest of the world, the stock market

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph below shows two levels of aggregate demand (AD) in Jabberton.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following changes would most likely lead to the shift from $AD_1$ to $AD_2$ shown in this graph?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid displays two downward-sloping straight lines. The vertical axis is labeled P L, and the horizontal axis is labeled real G D P. The line labeled A D 1 is to the right of the line labeled A D 2. A left-pointing arrow is placed between the two curves.

choices.content: An increase in real interest rates
An increase in expected future profits

assessment_items.question_content:
Aggregate demand (AD) has changed in Jabberton, as illustrated in the graph shown here.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following best describes a movement that would result from a decrease in personal income taxes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots two downward-sloping lines labeled A D 1 and A D 2. The vertical axis is labeled P L, and the horizontal axis is labeled real G D P. Points A and C lie on A D 1, while points B and D lie on A D 2. Dashed horizontal lines extend from points A and B to the vertical axis. Dashed vertical lines extend from points A, B, C, and D to the horizontal axis.

choices.content: A decrease in taxes would not cause any movement

assessment_items.question_content:
Aggregate demand (AD) in the United States has changed as shown in this graph.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/392d605e358da50a9577ee565498b6e965de7db0)

**Which of the following could cause the change shown?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in national income in a trading partner, such as the eurozone
An increase in the price level in the United States
A decrease in the price level in the United States
A decrease in investment spending in the United States
A decrease in government spending in the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shown here illustrates a change in the aggregate demand (AD) curve in Maxistan.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24b74fabc97bee88dcfc622e39fdb27a12a2eba5)

**Which of the following could cause the change shown?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

One of the reasons that aggregate demand (AD) is downward sloping is the interest rate effect.

**Which of the following best describes the interest rate effect?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in the price level will lead to an increase in interest rates
An increase in the money supply lowers interest rates
A decrease in the money supply lowers interest rates
An increase in the price level decreases the demand for money
A decrease in the price level increases real wealth

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does a decrease in the price level affect real wealth and aggregate demand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real wealth increases; the quantity of aggregate demand increases
Real wealth increases; aggregate demand increases
Real wealth decreases; the quantity of aggregate demand increases
Real wealth decreases; aggregate demand increases
Real wealth increases; the quantity of aggregate demand decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
When the price level increases, the amount of goods and services demanded economy-wide decreases because when the price level increases, everyone’s assets can purchase fewer goods and services.

**What is the term for the relationship described above?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

When the price level increases, goods in other countries are relatively cheaper. As a result, a country’s imports increase, displacing the demand for domestic production.

**What is the term for the relationship described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
